extraction.one_line_summary: DENZ Nico won stage 14 from a reduced breakaway sprint, while ARMIRAIL Bruno took pink at 19:30+ as first Frenchman to lead Giro since 1999.
tactical_patterns: INEOS controlled Sempione descent knowing 29-rider break threatened ARMIRAIL's 18:30 GC deficit, revealing preventable jersey theft calculation error.
BETTIOL Alberto initiated late break fragmentation from 29-man group, but launching 300m sprint on uphill finish burned matches too early.
DENZ won uphill finish two stages after first Giro victory, exposing repeatability advantage over one-dimensional sprinters in selective finales.

rider_signals.DENZ Nico: positive: won stage 14 with tactical discipline, waiting while BETTIOL burned early on 300m-to-go uphill sprint
rider_signals.GEE Derek: neutral: third consecutive runner-up finish reveals consistent positioning but chronic inability to convert final 200m
rider_signals.ARMIRAIL Bruno: positive: took pink at 19:30+ gap as first French maglia rosa since 1999, though likely one-day rental before mountain stages
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: attacked to form winning break but misjudged uphill sprint timing, launching 300m out with nothing left
rider_signals.BAIS Davide: positive: reclaimed mountains jersey by maximizing Sempione points after losing it previous day, showing active defense strategy
rider_signals.OLDANI Stefano: negative: won 2022 Genoa stage but couldn't replicate on similar selective finish, suggesting form regression
